---
title: "Tutorial 2 — A geospatial flow map on a globe"
---

::: {.lead}
Reproduce the kind of global flow map shown in the SciGraphs paper: nodes are
countries placed on a 3D globe, edges are geodesic great-circle arcs, and node
size encodes a flow volume.
:::

![Global refugee migration flows (UNHCR, 2000–2016): country nodes scaled by displaced population, connected by geodesic arcs on a textured globe, rendered in Cycles.](../assets/REFUGEES3.png)

**What you need:** an edge list whose source/target columns are place names
(countries or cities), ideally with a weight column for flow volume — or
explicit latitude/longitude columns.

## 1. Import with geocoding

1. Open **SciGraphs ▸ [Data](../panels/scigraphs/data.qmd)** and load your file
   (**File** source, set the delimiter, **Load File**).
2. Map **Source Nodes** / **Target Nodes** and set the **Weight Column** to your
   flow volume. Enable **Directed Graph** if the flows are directional.

## 2. Configure the globe

1. Expand the **[Geospatial Options](../panels/scigraphs/data.qmd#geospatial)**
   subpanel and enable it via its header checkbox.
2. Turn on **Auto-Geocode Source/Target as Countries/Cities** so the node names
   are resolved to coordinates via Nominatim. (Alternatively, disable geocoding
   and point the **Latitude**/**Longitude** fields at coordinate columns.)
3. Enable **Show Earth Globe**, set the **Globe Radius** and **Globe Quality**.
4. Choose a **Globe Theme** — e.g. **NASA Blue Marble** for satellite imagery —
   and tune the **PBR Settings** (water shine/roughness, land roughness, surface
   relief). Use **Pre-download** to cache the texture.
5. Set the **Edge Style** for the geodesic arcs.

## 3. Create the graph

Press **Create Graph** in **Step 3**, then **Setup Visual** in **Step 4**. The
nodes are placed on the globe and edges are drawn as great-circle arcs.

::: {.callout-note}
In geospatial mode the layout step is skipped automatically — positions come
from coordinates, not from a layout algorithm.
:::

## 4. Encode flow volume

1. In **[Visualization ▸ Appearance](../panels/scigraphs/visualization.qmd#appearance)**,
   drive **node size** from the weighted in-degree (total displaced population)
   using the attribute multiplier.
2. Colour nodes or arcs by origin/destination class or by volume with a
   scientific colormap.

## 5. Render

Frame the globe with the camera, add the lighting rig
(**[Scene Setup](../panels/scigraphs/visualization.qmd#scene)**), and render with
Cycles for path-traced imagery. Because the graph attributes (flow volumes,
classifications) remain accessible, you can re-style interactively without
re-projecting.

This workflow produces publication-quality imagery that is impractical to
achieve in conventional 2D network tools without exporting geometry to external
3D software.
